page: Amazon Web Services im4gn.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom ARM64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an AWS Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The hardware configuration is notable for its bundled 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance firmly in the average tier for most multi-core and database operations. It achieves a multi-core stress-ng score of 24284.34 and a Passmark database operations score of 10366.25. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 946.86 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, though uncached large-block writes drop to 73.19 GB/sec. Single-threaded performance shows some limitations, with weak ratings in Geekbench single-core (947.0) and Passmark CPU encryption (2177.61).

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the im4gn.4xlarge leverages the ARM64 Graviton architecture to deliver high local storage density and solid multi-threaded throughput without the premium associated with traditional x86 storage instances. The absence of a GPU and the moderate single-core performance represent clear tradeoffs, making it less suitable for heavy single-threaded tasks or complex AI model training.

This instance is highly suited for storage-intensive and database workloads, such as Redis deployments, where it achieves 18.89 million SET operations per second, and static web serving, which clocks at 18.93 GB/sec. It is also well-suited for multi-threaded decompression tasks, software compilation, and medium-scale database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated ARM64 physical cores on an AWS Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of memory and a substantial 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers balanced multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 24284.34 and 18.89 million Redis SET operations per second. While single-threaded performance and encryption benchmarks are relatively weak, its high storage density and solid memory bandwidth make it highly cost-efficient for storage-heavy workloads. The im4gn.4xlarge is best suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.4xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 server featuring 16 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.4xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 instance with 16 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSDs and balanced multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 physical cores on AWS Neoverse-N1 at 2.5 GHz
7500 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
64.0 GB of system memory with 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong multi-threaded decompression and database benchmark performance
Optimized for Redis, static web serving, and storage-intensive workloads
